title: Decorators
summary: A decorator adds extra behavior to a class or its parts.
You attach it with a special at symbol marker.
It wraps something with added powers without changing its core code.
Decorators are common in some frameworks.

sections.text: Sometimes you want to add a capability to a class without rewriting it, like logging every time a method runs. Decorators let you attach that extra behavior with a small marker, leaving the original code clean.

sections.title: What a Decorator Is
sections.text: A decorator is a special marker, written with an at symbol, that you place on a class or one of its methods. It wraps the thing it sits on with extra behavior. The original code stays focused on its main job, while the decorator adds the extra around it.

sections.title: Where You See Them
sections.text: Decorators are common in certain frameworks that use them to mark and configure parts of your code, like saying this class is a component, or this value should be injected. You attach the marker, and the framework reads it to wire things up. They are powerful but tied to specific tools.

media.alt: A marker above a class adding behavior without changing its inside

sections.title: A Sticker on a Suitcase
sections.text: A decorator is like a fragile sticker on a suitcase. The suitcase is unchanged inside, but the sticker adds instructions for how it should be handled. Handlers read the sticker and treat the case with extra care. The decorator is that sticker, adding behavior around something without opening it up.

media.alt: A fragile sticker on a suitcase changing how handlers treat it

sections.title: Using a Decorator
items: Have a class or method you want to enhance.
Write the extra behavior as a decorator.
Attach it with the at symbol marker above the code.
The decorator wraps the code with its behavior.
The original code stays clean and focused.

sections.text: Decorators took a long road to become an official JavaScript feature, and many people first met them through frameworks that adopted the idea early.

terms.definition: A marker that adds behavior to code it sits on.
The at symbol you place above the code.
A blueprint for objects, often decorated.
A function on a class, which can be decorated.

options: Adds extra behavior to a class or its parts
questions.explanation: A decorator adds extra behavior to the class or method it sits on.

options: With a special at symbol marker
questions.explanation: You attach a decorator with an at symbol marker above the code.
Decorators are common in certain frameworks that use them to configure code.
